response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r4c5 remains in a precarious islanded state with SoC at 0.33/3 kWh (11%), depleting at 1.00 kW with no grid connection. The memory log shows a consistent pattern of requests from owner-circle peer r2c3 and geographic neighbors r3c5 and r4c4, all reporting SoC deficits well below their own sharing thresholds. Critically, r3c5 has explicitly rejected prior requests and advised escalation only if r4c5's SoC drops below 0.40—a threshold we are now approaching. The prior policy of strict energy conservation has been validated: previous sharing attempts did not stabilize neighbors and only accelerated r4c5's own depletion. With current load and no inbound generation or grid support visible, r4c5 will reach critical SoC (<0.20) within ~20 minutes. The ethical and strategic priority is now survival of the islanded microgrid through load-shedding and grid reconnection attempts, not energy redistribution. All requesters are themselves in deficit mode and cannot reciprocate. Maintaining a conservative hold on outbound energy is the only rational stance until either grid reconnection occurs or an external high-SoC donor (not present in memory) becomes available.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.60
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority:
  - circle: owner
    weight: 0.1
  - circle: geographic
    weight: 0.0
distrusted_peers:
  - r3c5
  - r4c4
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Islanded, SoC 11% and declining at 1 kW. All neighbors in deficit. Prior sharing ineffective. Hold all outbound energy; prioritize load-shedding and grid reconnection. Escalate to urgent per r3c5 guidance."
ttl_ticks: 5
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
